skill.name: error-tracking-integrator
skill.description: Adds comprehensive error tracking with Sentry, Rollbar, or similar services including error boundaries, context, and breadcrumbs. Use when user requests error monitoring or mentions production debugging.
skill.summary: Integrates application error tracking, alerting, context, breadcrumbs, and source map support.
skill.author: CrazyDubya

security_audit.summary: The static Ruby backtick and weak cryptography findings are false positives caused by Markdown code fences and ordinary prose in SKILL.md. The real risk is medium because the skill instructs users to add third-party telemetry, user context, breadcrumbs, and source map upload tokens.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party Error Telemetry Can Include User Data
medium_findings.description: The skill recommends Sentry, Rollbar, Bugsnag, Airbrake, and LogRocket, then shows examples that attach user email, username, order context, breadcrumbs, and captured exceptions. This is legitimate observability guidance, but it can send personal or business data to external services if users do not scrub events carefully.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The telemetry examples clearly include identifiers and application context. The skill also includes a data scrubbing example, so the issue is a managed privacy risk rather than malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Source Map Upload Requires Sensitive Auth Token
medium_findings.description: The source map guidance uses SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN for production uploads. This is normal Sentry configuration, but token handling must be limited to build environments and must not be committed to source control.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The auth token reference is explicit and located in production source map configuration. No exfiltration code is present, but secret handling risk is real.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Misidentified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported Ruby or shell backtick execution across many lines, but the evidence is Markdown fenced code blocks and JavaScript examples. No Ruby backtick operator or dynamic shell execution is present in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reported locations align with Markdown fences and inline template filenames. Direct review found no executable Ruby shell backtick usage.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Access Is Configuration-Oriented
low_findings.description: The process.env references configure DSNs, environment names, and a source map upload token. They are expected for error tracking setup and do not read environment files or send secrets outside the configured tracking workflow.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The environment variable usage is visible in small configuration snippets. No evidence of environment file reading or broad secret harvesting was found.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography and Reconnaissance Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alert on the front matter description and the reconnaissance alert near the captureException example do not correspond to cryptographic code or system enumeration. No evidence found for these static labels.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of both lines shows descriptive metadata and an orderId field. Neither location contains cryptographic algorithm use or host reconnaissance.

dangerous_patterns.title: Telemetry Context May Contain Personal Data
dangerous_patterns.description: Examples show user and order context being attached to error events. Users should minimize event payloads and apply beforeSend filtering before production rollout.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The cited snippets explicitly include user identifiers and order context. The risk depends on deployment policy and event filtering.

content.value_statement: Production errors are hard to diagnose when reports lack context and release details. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through adding error tracking, context, alerts, and privacy filters.
actual_capabilities: Detects common frontend and backend frameworks before recommending integration steps.
Provides Sentry setup examples for React and Node.js Express applications.
Shows how to add React error boundaries for captured UI failures.
Demonstrates user context, tags, custom context, breadcrumbs, and manual exception capture.
Includes guidance for source maps, alert configuration, performance monitoring, and event filtering.
limitations: It gives implementation guidance but does not create accounts or projects in external tracking services.
Examples focus mainly on Sentry, with other services listed at a higher level.
Privacy controls require project-specific review before sending production events.
The referenced template files are listed but are not present in the scanned skill package.

use_cases.title: Add frontend crash reporting
use_cases.description: Install browser error tracking, wrap the app with an error boundary, and capture useful UI breadcrumbs.

use_cases.title: Monitor backend exceptions
use_cases.description: Add server-side error handlers, environment-specific DSNs, and structured exception context for production APIs.

use_cases.title: Prepare release debugging
use_cases.description: Configure source maps, release tags, alert rules, and team notification practices for new deployments.

prompt_templates.title: Add basic Sentry setup
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a single application
prompt_templates.prompt: Add basic Sentry error tracking to this project. Detect the framework, install the needed package, and configure the DSN from environment variables.

prompt_templates.title: Add UI error boundaries
prompt_templates.prompt: Add a React error boundary with a clear fallback screen. Capture errors in Sentry and keep the fallback accessible.

prompt_templates.title: Add production context
prompt_templates.prompt: Add useful Sentry tags, breadcrumbs, and manual exception capture for this workflow. Avoid sending passwords, tokens, or unnecessary personal data.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare observability rollout
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a production error tracking rollout plan. Include source maps, release tracking, alert rules, sample rates, privacy filters, and verification steps.

output_examples.input: Add error tracking to my React app.
output_examples.output: The assistant identifies React, installs the Sentry React package, adds initialization with an environment DSN, and wraps the app in an error boundary.

output_examples.input: Production checkout errors are hard to debug.
output_examples.output: The assistant adds targeted exception capture for checkout, attaches safe order metadata, and recommends alerts for payment and regression errors.

output_examples.input: Prepare source maps for release debugging.
output_examples.output: The assistant updates build configuration for source map uploads, uses a build-only auth token, and recommends release tagging for readable traces.

best_practices: Use separate DSNs or projects for development, staging, and production environments.
Filter passwords, tokens, and personal data before events leave the application.
Tag releases and upload source maps from a controlled build environment.
anti_patterns: Do not send full request bodies or session payloads to an error tracking service.
Do not commit DSNs with secret properties or source map auth tokens to source control.
Do not enable maximum performance tracing in production without a sampling policy.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it only support Sentry?
faq.answer: No. It lists Sentry, Rollbar, Bugsnag, Airbrake, and LogRocket, but the detailed examples focus on Sentry.

faq.question: Can it add React error boundaries?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes a React error boundary example using Sentry's ErrorBoundary component.

faq.question: Does it handle sensitive data?
faq.answer: It includes a beforeSend filtering example, but teams must adapt filtering rules to their data policy.

faq.question: Can it configure source maps?
faq.answer: Yes. It shows source map configuration with a Sentry Webpack plugin and an auth token from environment variables.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a medium risk warning for third-party telemetry, environment secrets, and privacy review requirements.
